title: astragalus calvertii
intro: Astragalus calvertii, also known as the Tibetan milk vetch, is a plant species within the legume family, characterized by its unique adaptation to high-altitude environments in the Himalayan regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its rich content of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Astragalus calvertii has been traditionally used to enhance immune function, reduce fatigue, and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory and cardiovascular diseases, making it a valuable herb in traditional Chinese medicine. In herbal preparations, the root of Astragalus calvertii is often dried and processed into a powder or extract, which can be consumed as a supplement or added to teas and infusions for its medicinal benefits.
